# @byted-service/logger

[![bnpm](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/badge/v/@byted-service/logger.svg?style=flat-square)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/logger) [![download](https://2y8ngapf.fn.bytedance.net/d?p=@byted-service/logger)](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@byted-service/logger)

[简体中文](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logger/README.md) | [English](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logger/README.en.md) | [CHANGELOG](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logger/CHANGELOG.md)

> The following is **@byted-service/logger@2.x** document, **1.x** document see [logger-v1.x](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/logger-v1.x/packages/logger), [@byted-service/logger@2.0.0 release log](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnwTrFMBrI0oZaYHxybzW0je)

**@byted-service/logger** is the company's basic logging module. The main features are as follows:

-   Output logs that meet the company's internal log specifications, see [Node.js log usage guide - @bytedservice/logger](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcnsLn77LSSKg996gVsKapAXg).
-   Supports [@byted-service/logagent](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logagent/README.md) to report logs to the `MS platform`.

If the framework you are using has integrated logger. It is not recommended to use this module directly, you can directly use the provided plugins.

-   Gulu: [@gulu/access-log](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/gulu/tree/master/packages/byted-gulu-access-log).
-   Koa & Express: [@fe/middlewares](https://web-bnpm.byted.org/package/@fe/middlewares).

## Installation

```bash
npm install @byted-service/logger --registry=http://bnpm.byted.org
```

## Quick start

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.info('hello world');
// The log formatting rules are the same as util.format.
logger.info('hello %s', 'world');
logger.log({
    level: 'info',
    message: 'hello %s',
    splat: ['world'],
});
```

Tips: [util.format](https://nodejs.org/dist/latest/docs/api/util.html#util_util_format_format_args)

## API

### new Logger([options])

-   `options`
    -   `level`: Log ouput level, the default value is `debug`. Logs below this level will not be processed.
    -   `defaultLevel`: Default log level, the default value is `info`.
    -   `streams`: Log output stream, see [Streams](#streams)
    -   `flushInterval`: Specify the log refresh interval, the default value is `10s`.
    -   `consoleLogLevel`: `console` log level, the default value is `options.level`.
    -   `fileLogLevel`: `file` log level, the default value is `options.level`.
    -   `agentLogLevel`: `logagent` log level, the default value is `options.level`.
    -   `metricsLevel`: `metrics` level, the default value is `warn`.
    -   `enableConsoleLog`: Whether to enable the `console` log, the default value is `isCloudIDE() || !isInTCE()` (Includes local development environment, Cloud IDE and Devbox), see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).
    -   `enableFileLog`: Whether to open the `file` log, the default value is `true`
    -   `enableAgentLog`: Whether to enable the `logagent` log, the default value is `!(isCloudIDE() || !isInTCE())`
    -   `enableMetrics`: Whether to enable `metrics`, the default value is `true`.
    -   `psm`: Project `psm`, the default value is `env.getPSM()`, see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).
    -   `prefix`: The default `prefix` of log messages.
    -   `tags`: Global log `tags`.
    -   `tagKeys`: Log `tagKeys`, used to quickly serialize log `tags`.
    -   `callSiteFrameIndex`: The stack depth is used to obtain the call log location. If you need to perform upper packaging based on `@byted-service/logger`, please pay attention to setting this value.
    -   `enableSecMark`: Whether to enable the `security mark`, the default value is `false`. At the `TTP` environment, the default value is `true`. After enabling it, the `key=value` will be converted to `{{key=value}}`. See [TTP environment usage guide - @byted-service/logger](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn9yu4qGfXObjt40GN8ni9Lu).
    -   `file`: The log file, when it is an array, will try to create the file one by one unit the file is writable.
    -   `rotatePattern`: Log cutting `cron` expressions, **only part of cron expressions are implemented, please use the beta function with caution**, cut once every hour by default.
        -   `0 0 0 * *`: Cut once every day.
        -   `0 0 * * *`: Cut once every hour.
        -   `0 * * * *`: Cut once every minute.
        -   `0 0 0,12 * *`: Cut at 0 o'clock and 12 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 0-3 * *`: Cut from 0 o'clock to 3 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 *\/2 * *`: Cutting even hours every day.
    -   `fileFormatter`: Log cutting file `formatter`, the default format is `/path/to/p.s.m.log.YYYY-MM-DD_HH:mm:ss`.
        -   `file: Original log file.
        -   `date`: Corresponding cutting date.
    -   `dir`: Log storage directory, when `env.isTest() || env.isProd()` is `true` (`false` in the local development environment), the default value is `/opt/tiger/toutiao/log/app`, otherwise the default value is `${process.cwd()}/log`, see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).
    -   `filename`: The name of the log file, the default value is `options.psm`.
    -   `ext`: The log file suffix, the default value is `.log`.
        -   If it is an access log, fill in `.access.log`.
        -   If it is an `rpc` log, fill in `.call.log`.
    -   `fallbackDirs`: The attempted directory after the log directory creation fails, the default value is `` [`${process.cwd()}/log`, `${os.tmpdir()}/log`] ``. If all fail, the creation of the log instance fails.
    -   `disableDirFallbackWarning`: Whether to turn off the output when the log directory creation fails, the default value is `false`.
    -   `agent`: `logagent` construction parameters, see [@byted-service/logagent](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logagent/README.en.md).
    -   `taskName`: `logagent` log type mark, `_rpc` means `rpc` log, the default value is `options.psm`.
    -   `metrics`: `metrics` construction parameter, or `metrics` instance, see [@byted-service/metrics](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/metrics/README.en.md).

### logger.level

Log output level, the default value is `options.level`, which can be set dynamically.

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.level = 'info';
logger.info('hello world at info level'); // Output.

logger.level = 'warn';
logger.info('hello world at warn level'); // Not output.
```

Tips: Because the `API` is too flexible, it is not recommended to use it directly.

#### logger.levels

Supported log level, the default value is

```js
{
    trace: 10,
    debug: 20,
    info: 30,
    warn: 40,
    error: 50,
    fatal: 60,
}
```

#### logger.defaultLevel

Default log level, the default value is `options.defaultLevel`.

#### logger.tags

Global log `tags`, the default value is `options.tags`, used as an index to query on the `MS platform`.

#### logger.tagKeys

Log `tagKeys`, used to quickly serialize log `tags` to improve performance.

#### logger.isLevelEnabled(level)

Whether the log level is turned on.

#### logger.fatal(message, [...splat])

#### logger.error(message, [...splat])

#### logger.warn(message, [...splat])

#### logger.info(message, [...splat])

#### logger.debug(message, [...splat])

#### logger.trace(message, [...splat])

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.fatal('hello world');
logger.error('hello world');
logger.warn('hello world');
logger.info('hello world');
logger.debug('hello world');
logger.trace('hello world');
```

#### logger.log([args])

-   args
    -   level: Log level, the default value is `options.defaultlevel`.
    -   message: Log content.
    -   splat: Log formatting parameters.
    -   logId: Log `logId`, usually completed automatically by the framework, see [x-tt-logid](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn2L5x9IgQ4iLEdHofSMPVQc#qUpuqi).
    -   location: The log call stack location, which is automatically calculated by default.
    -   tags: Log `tags`, merged with `options.tags` in the form of `Object.assign()`, used as an index for query on the `MS platform`.

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.log({
    level: 'info',
    message: 'hello %s',
    splat: ['world'],
    logId: 'some logid',
    location: 'some location',
    tags: {
        some: 'tags',
    },
});
```

#### logger.flush()

Flush the log asynchronously to [Streams](#streams), by default it is automatically refreshed according to `options.flushInterval`.

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.info('hello world');
logger.flush();
```

#### logger.flushSync()

Flush the log synchronously to [Streams](#streams).

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.info('hello world');
logger.flushSync();
```

#### logger.clone([options])

Copy log instance, a lightweight copy, and share the same with the original `logger` [Streams](#streams) by default.

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();
const childLogger = logger.clone();

childLogger.info('hello world');
```

#### logger.addStream(level, stream)

Add [Streams](#streams).

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

logger.addStream('info', {
    write(msg) {
        console.log(msg);
    },
});

logger.info('hello world');
```

#### logger.deleteStream(level, stream)

Delete [Streams](#streams).

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();

const stream = {
    write(msg) {
        console.log(msg);
    },
};
logger.addStream('info', stream);
logger.deleteStream('info', stream);

logger.info('hello world');
```

### logger.getFinalLogger()

Obtain a synchronous logger, which can be used in situations where it is impossible to write logs asynchronously. E.g:

-   process.on('uncaughtException')
-   process.on('unhandledRejection')

Please do not use console.log directly, because whether console.log is synchronized or not depends on the environment. For details, see the document: https://nodejs.org/dist/latest-v12.x/docs/api/process.html#process_a_note_on_process_i_o

-   Only applicable to file log writing. `logagent` are not supported.

```js
const { Logger } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new Logger();
const finalLogger = logger.getFinalLogger();

process.on('uncaughtException', (err) => {
    finalLogger.error(err, 'uncaughtException');
    process.exit(1);
});
```

### Streams

Log output stream. In `@byted-service/logger`, four log output streams, `console`, `file`, `logagent`, and `metrcis`, are built in by default.

-   If you use [new Logger(\[options\])](#new-loggeroptions), the above log output stream will be added by default, which can be controlled by parameters and environment variables.
-   If you use [new CoreLogger(\[options\])](#new-coreloggeroptions), it can be controlled by `logger.addStream()` and `logger.deleteStream()`.

#### createConsole()

Create `console` output stream.

```js
const { CoreLogger, createConsole } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new CoreLogger();

logger.addStream('info', createConsole());

logger.info('hello world');
```

#### createFileRotate([options])

Create `file` output stream, **compatible with the old version of `logger`**, see [@byted-service/logger upgrade instructions](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn5dsQLTFYuGBdbB7ULRFHpb#99sYNJ).

-   `options`
    -   `psm`: Project `psm`, the default value is `env.getPSM()`, see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).
    -   `file`: The log file, when it is an array, will try to create the file one by one unit the file is writable.
    -   `rotatePattern`: Log cutting `cron` expressions, **only part of cron expressions are implemented, please use the beta function with caution**, cut once every hour by default.
        -   `0 0 0 * *`: Cut once every day.
        -   `0 0 * * *`: Cut once every hour.
        -   `0 * * * *`: Cut once every minute.
        -   `0 0 0,12 * *`: Cut at 0 o'clock and 12 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 0-3 * *`: Cut from 0 o'clock to 3 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 *\/2 * *`: Cutting even hours every day.
    -   `fileFormatter`: Log cutting file `formatter`, the default format is `/path/to/p.s.m.log.YYYY-MM-DD_HH:mm:ss`.
        -   `file: Original log file.
        -   `date`: Corresponding cutting date.
    -   `dir`: Log storage directory, when `env.isTest() || env.isProd()` is `true` (`false` in the local development environment), the default value is `/opt/tiger/toutiao/log/app`, otherwise the default value is `${process.cwd()}/log`, see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).
    -   `filename`: The name of the log file, the default value is `options.psm`.
    -   `ext`: The log file suffix, the default value is `.log`.
        -   If it is an access log, fill in `.access.log`.
        -   If it is an `rpc` log, fill in `.call.log`.
    -   `fallbackDirs`: The attempted directory after the log directory creation fails, the default value is `` [`${process.cwd()}/log`, `${os.tmpdir()}/log`] ``. If all fail, the creation of the log instance fails.
    -   `disableDirFallbackWarning`: Whether to turn off the output when the log directory creation fails, the default value is `false`.

```js
const { CoreLogger, createFileRotate } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new CoreLogger();

logger.addStream('info', createFileRotate({
    file: '/path/to/xxx.log';
}));

logger.info('hello world');
```

#### createLogAgent([options])

Create `logagent` output stream.

-   `options`
    -   `agent`: `logagent` construction parameters, see [@byted-service/logagent](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/logagent/README.en.md).
    -   `taskName`: `logagent` log type mark, `_rpc` means `rpc` log, the default value is `env.getPSM()`, see [@byted-service/env](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/tree/master/packages/env/README.en.md).

```js
const { CoreLogger, createLogAgent } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new CoreLogger();

logger.addStream('info', createLogAgent());

logger.info('hello world');
```

#### createMetrics([options])

Create `metrics` output stream.

-   `options`
    -   `metrics`: `metrics` construction parameter, or `metrics` instance, see [@byted-service/metrics](https://code.byted.org/nodejs/byted-service/blob/master/packages/metrics/README.en.md).

```js
const { CoreLogger, createLogAgent } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new CoreLogger();

logger.addStream('info', createMetrics());

logger.info('hello world');
```

#### new FileRotateStream(options)

Create `file` output stream, **not compatible with the old version of `logger`**, see [@byted-service/logger upgrade instructions](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn5dsQLTFYuGBdbB7ULRFHpb#99sYNJ).

-   `options`
    -   `file`: The log file, when it is an array, will try to create the file one by one unit the file is writable.
    -   `rotatePattern`: Log cutting `cron` expressions, **only part of cron expressions are implemented, please use the beta function with caution**, cut once every hour by default.
        -   `0 0 0 * *`: Cut once every day.
        -   `0 0 * * *`: Cut once every hour.
        -   `0 * * * *`: Cut once every minute.
        -   `0 0 0,12 * *`: Cut at 0 o'clock and 12 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 0-3 * *`: Cut from 0 o'clock to 3 o'clock every day.
        -   `0 0 *\/2 * *`: Cutting even hours every day.
    -   `fileFormatter`: Log cutting file `formatter`, the default format is `/path/to/p.s.m.log.YYYY-MM-DD_HH:mm:ss`.
        -   `file: Original log file.
        -   `date`: Corresponding cutting date.

```js
const { CoreLogger, FileRotateStream } = require('@byted-service/logger');
const logger = new CoreLogger();

logger.addStream(
    'info',
    new FileRotateStream({
        file: '/path/to/xxx.log',
    })
);

logger.info('hello world');
```

### new CoreLogger([options])

The core of `logger`, which can be used to customize the implementation of `logger` to the maximum extent.

-   `options`
    -   `level`: Log ouput level, the default value is `debug`. Logs below this level will not be processed.
    -   `defaultLevel`: Default log level, the default value is `info`.
    -   `formatter`: Log formatter.
    -   `streams`: Log output stream, see [Streams](#streams)
    -   `flushInterval`: Specify the log refresh interval, the default value is `10s`.

## Environment variable

You can control the log output without modifying the code by corresponding environment variables.

### Log level

-   Optional value ( values not in the range of optional values will be directly ignored ).

    -   `fatal`: Serious errors that make the program unusable.
    -   `error`: For a serious error of an operation, but the program is not affected.
    -   `warn`: Problems that may cause the program to enter an abnormal state.
    -   `info`: Normal information ( such as service start/stop ).
    -   `debug`: Information for program debugging.
    -   `trace`: Information used to track program operation or request call chain.

-   Level size: `fatal` > `error` > `warn` > `info` > `debug` > `trace`

Related environment variables:

-   `LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL`: The default log level, logs below this level will not be processed.
-   `LOGGER_LEVEL_CONSOLE`: `console` log level.
-   `LOGGER_LEVEL_FILE`: `file` log level.
-   `LOGGER_LEVEL_LOGAGENT`: `logagent` log level.
-   `LOGGER_LEVEL_METRICS`: `metrics` log level.

```shell
# At this time, all logs below the error level will not be output
LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL=error node app.js
```

Parameter priority:

-   General log level: `options.level` > `LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL` > `info level`
-   `console` log level: `options.consoleLogLevel` > `LOGGER_LEVEL_CONSOLE` > `options.level` > `LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL` > `info level`
-   `file` log level: `options.fileLogLevel` > `LOGGER_LEVEL_FILE` > `options.level` > `LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL` > `info level`
-   `logagent` log level: `options.agentLogLevel` > `LOGGER_LEVEL_LOGAGENT` > `options.level` > `LOGGER_OUTPUT_LEVEL` > `info level`
-   `metrics` log level: `options.metricsLevel` > `LOGGER_LEVEL_METRICS` > `warn level`

### Log component

-   Optional value ( values not in the range of optional values will be directly ignored )
    -   `true`: Open log.
    -   `false`: Close log.

Related environment variables:

-   `LOGGER_ENABLE_CONSOLE`: Whether to enable the console log.
-   `LOGGER_ENABLE_FILE`: Whether to enable file log.
-   `LOGGER_ENABLE_LOGAGENT`: Whether to enable logagent log.
-   `LOGGER_ENABLE_METRICS`: Whether to enable metrics log.

```shell
# At this time, all logagent logs will not be output
LOGGER_ENABLE_LOGAGENT=false node app.js
```

Parameter priority:

-   `console` log: `options.enableConsoleLog` > `LOGGER_ENABLE_CONSOLE` > `default rule`
-   `file` log: `options.enableFileLog` > `LOGGER_ENABLE_FILE` > `default rule`
-   `logagent` log: `options.enableAgentLog` > `LOGGER_ENABLE_LOGAGENT` > `default rule`
-   `metrics` log: `options.enableMetrics` > `LOGGER_ENABLE_METRICS` > `default rule`

The default rule is as follows：

![](./images/1.jpg)

### Log Timezone

Related environment variables:

-   `LOGGER_TIME_ZONE`: Log Timezone.

```shell
# At this time, the log timezone is America/New_York.
LOGGER_TIME_ZONE=America/New_York node app.js
```

### Security Mark

Related environment variables:

-   `LOGGER_ENABLE_SEC_MARK`: Whether to enable `Security Mark` for `TTP` environment, see [TTP environment usage guide - @byted-service/logger](https://bytedance.feishu.cn/wiki/wikcn9yu4qGfXObjt40GN8ni9Lu).

```shell
# Close Security Mark.
LOGGER_ENABLE_SEC_MARK=false node app.js
```

### Logger Hook

From the `>2.4.0` version logger supports the hook function, which is used for business processing log printing content. For example, sensitive information is not printed in the log. The usage method is as follows:

```typescript
import { Logger, InputData } from '@byted-service/logger';

const logger = new Logger();

logger.addHook((input: InputData) => {
    input.message = input.message.replace(/\d+/, '****');
    return input;
});

logger.info('hello 123456'); // hello ****
```

## Metrics

Indicator name:

```
// Log call count, can be modified by options.metrics parameter
toutiao.service.log.${psm}.throughput
```
